program_name: Economics and Management of Arts and Cultural Activities - Economics and Administration of Arts and Culture

academic_requirements.summary: A minimum three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent is required. Applicants need a coherent academic background in arts/culture or economics/business, with specific complementary ECTS requirements. The current apply.unive.it international course page also states a minimum CGPA of 75% [uncertain].
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree equivalent to an Italian three-year bachelor's degree (at least 3 years / 180 ECTS or equivalent).
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: If the previous degree is in Cultural Heritage or disciplines related to visual arts and music, the applicant must also have at least 12 ECTS in Economics, Management or Statistics. If the previous degree is in Business Administration or Economics, the applicant must also have at least 12 ECTS in Art History. If the previous degree is in another field, the applicant must have at least 42 ECTS overall, including at least 12 ECTS in Economics/Management/Statistics, at least 12 ECTS in Art History, and at least 12 ECTS in Architecture, Law, Roman History, Italian Literature, Archaeology and Art History, Aesthetics, Geography, History, Sociology, Ethnography, Anthropology, Informatics, International Law, or Christian and Medieval Archaeology.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: [uncertain] The current apply.unive.it international course page states a minimum CGPA of 75%. The current 2026/2027 unive.it programme admission page does not restate this CGPA threshold.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: Applicants with international qualifications must first pass the Welcome Unit preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it. The programme Teaching Committee then evaluates the undergraduate transcript of records and the CV to verify curricular coherence.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certification accepted by Ca' Foscari and submitted within 5 years from the certification date.

Examples on the official recognised list include Cambridge English FCE, CAE and CPE; Trinity ISE II-IV; IELTS; TOEFL iBT; PTE Academic; Oxford Test of English; LanguageCert ESOL; Aptis ESOL; and Ca' Foscari CLA English certificates.
exemption_rules: Completed higher education studies, or at least one year of college, in a country where English is the medium of instruction.
Obtained a high-school diploma or university qualification in Italy or abroad after studies taught entirely in English.
Completed at least one full exchange year in high school at an institution where English was the language of instruction.
Holds one of the specific Italian university language degrees listed by Ca' Foscari for English-language exemption.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Upload the certificate electronically during the application/enrolment process. Ca' Foscari does not run an English test as part of the application procedure for this programme.
language_requirements.deadline: The certificate may be missing at application stage, but it must be provided to complete enrolment by the enrolment deadline.

rounds.round_name: 2026/2027 master's admission rounds for EU and equivalent applicants on apply.unive.it
rounds.application_window: Applications open from December 17, 2025 [uncertain round-by-round breakdown].
rounds.deadline: Final deadline for all master's degrees: June 17, 2026.
rounds.notes: The public EU-category apply.unive.it article exposes the final master's deadline but not the detailed round breakdown in the accessible text.

application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: [uncertain] The public EU-category apply.unive.it article does not state the programme enrolment deadline. The programme admission page for the degree lists enrolment from July 1 to September 30, 2026, with an additional late period from October 1 to December 18, 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: This field is based on the official apply.unive.it admission-rounds article for EU/equivalent applicants, which is the official source named in Ca' Foscari's preliminary-evaluation pages for international applicants.

rounds.round_name: 2026/2027 master's admission rounds for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa
rounds.application_window: [uncertain] The public visa-applicant article does not expose the individual round windows in the accessible text.
rounds.deadline: As of June 5, 2026, the official article states that it is no longer possible to apply for admission to a bachelor's or master's degree in 2026/2027.
rounds.notes: Non-EU applicants already residing in Italy with a permit valid for enrolment are instructed to refer instead to the EU/equivalent admission-rounds article.

application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: [uncertain] Not stated in the accessible public visa-applicant article.
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Closed as of June 5, 2026 according to the official apply.unive.it article for visa-requiring applicants.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The source is current-status guidance for the visa-requiring category rather than a round-by-round schedule.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: European applicants may use an ID card; non-European applicants use a passport.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or receipt of renewal/first request
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records for all undergraduate years
required_documents.notes: A provisional transcript is acceptable if the applicant is still attending the final year.

required_documents.document_name: Official English or Italian translations of transcript and diploma documents
required_documents.notes: Required when the original documents are not in English or Italian.

required_documents.document_name: Grading scale of the university CGPA
required_documents.notes: Listed as highly recommended on the official international-entry-requirements page.
Used by the Teaching Committee when assessing curricular coherence.
A dedicated motivation letter for this master's degree must be added to the application.
Optional during the application stage if not yet available, but required later for final enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: CIMEA Statement of Comparability, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondence with CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement
required_documents.notes: These documents are strongly recommended during application and are mandatory later to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Additional detailed syllabus or programme description
required_documents.notes: May be requested later by the Teaching Committee.

entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Personal preparation is assessed through curriculum vitae and studiorum, with a possible additional interview by the Teaching Committee.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed written-test dates are published for this programme on the current admission pages.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: No test score threshold is published. On the current programme admission page, candidates with a final mark of 95/110 in the listed bachelor's degree classes are generally considered to have the necessary personal preparation.

source_quotes.quote: Economics and Management of Arts and Cultural Activities - Economics and Administration of Arts and Culture
a minimum CGPA (Cumulative Grade Point Average) of 75%
A minimum B2-level knowledge of English is mandatory for enrolment.
A motivation letter must be added to your application.

uncertainty_notes: The current apply.unive.it international course page still frames some admissions content as pending confirmation for 2026/2027; the CGPA threshold of 75% is therefore kept with an [uncertain] marker.
The public apply.unive.it article for EU/equivalent applicants provides the final master's deadline of June 17, 2026, but the accessible text does not expose the detailed round-by-round breakdown.
The public apply.unive.it article for visa-requiring applicants gives current closure status, but not a detailed visa round schedule or a specific visa/pre-enrolment deadline.
